rulings.Case Title: SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC. et al VS. TCC STEEL, A KOREAN COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 5, 2008, line 12. PLAINTIFFS SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC., DELTA BUILDING PRODUCTS, LTD. AND OMEGA ROOF, INC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The Court vacates its order of February 13, 2008 consolidating cases 06-451766 and 07-463981. Plaintiffs in the '07 case are solely responsible for this result since they insisted on naming plaintiff's counsel in the '06 case as a defendant in the '07 case. The obvious result of doing so would be to delay the trial of the consolidated cases on August 18, 2008. By vacating the order of consolidation, the '06 case may proceed to trial. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSITICE FOUNDATION VS. THE FAUCET-QUEENS, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Winner International, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 5, 2008, line 5. PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSITICE FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Winner International, LLC. A hearing is required. Counsel to address: Why it will take 270 days for a warning to be included with the sale of the product? What are the differences between defendants that result in different civil penalties? Should the Court request plaintiff to identify non-profits that benefit by reference to the dollars received and by whom over a stated period? =(302/PJM/xAH)

rulings.Case Title: JACKSON ROYBAL et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDIAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 5, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Given the significance of the ruling, the Court granted plaintiff's request for oral argument. The Court continues to find that plaintiff cannot state a claim for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Thing v. La Chusa (1989) 48 Cal. 3d 644; Bird v. Saenz (2002) 28 Cal 4th 910 and Goldstein v. Superior Court (1990) 223 Cal. App. 3d 1415. The complaint fails to allege facts that provide a basis for the conclusion that a lay person could percieve a misdiagnosis or failure to diagnose the presence of a sponge in the child's abdomen. See Meighan v. Shore (1995) 34 Cal. App. 4th 1025. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SST INVESTMENTS LLC VS. UVALDO RAMIREZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Uvaldo Ramirez, Byron Ramirez, Augustin Garcia, Mario Morales' Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 5, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANTS UVALDO RAMIREZ, BYRON RAMIREZ, AUGUSTIN GARCIA AND MARIO MORALES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Defendants are not engaged in a nuisance simply by occupying a unit based on a landlord-tenant relationship. The landlord is required to maintain the premises in a habitable condition. In doing so, the landlord is obligated to protect the rights of the tenants. See Rent Ordinance section 37.9 (11) and 37.9c. =(302/PJM/LB)
